# Typesense Action-Based RBAC with Fine-Grained API Keys

## Background

Typesense is an open-source, typo-tolerant search engine. Its Keys API can mint fine-grained API keys that are restricted on a per-action and per-collection basis. In this task you will run a standalone Typesense server, use the bootstrap admin key to provision several role-specific keys, and confirm that each key is limited to exactly the operations it was granted (and rejected for everything else). This is about action-based RBAC across distinct keys, not cryptographically scoped single-tenant search keys.

## Requirements

* Run a standalone Typesense server (native Linux binary) on port 8108 with bootstrap API key `xyz`.
* Create two collections, `products` and `orders`. Each must have a searchable string field named `title`. Index at least one document into each collection.
* Using the Keys API, create the following three fine-grained keys with the narrowest scopes that satisfy each role:
  * A **search-only** key that may search any collection, but cannot write documents or manage collections/keys.
  * A **documents-write** key that may write documents (create, upsert, and import) into ONLY the `products` collection, and cannot search and cannot write to any other collection.
  * An **admin** key that may perform all operations on all collections.
* Persist the full generated key values, because Typesense only returns the full key string once, in the creation response.

## Implementation Hints

* Send the bootstrap key `xyz` via the `X-TYPESENSE-API-KEY` header to create the collections and the role keys.
* The `actions` and `collections` arrays on each key control what it may do; choose scopes appropriately (wildcards are allowed where a role legitimately needs them).
* Project path: /home/user/typesense-rbac
* Run Typesense with data directory `/home/user/typesense-rbac/typesense-data`, api key `xyz`, on port `8108`, and confirm it is healthy (`GET /health` returns `{"ok":true}`) before using it.
* Because only the creation response contains the full key value, write the three key values to `/home/user/typesense-rbac/keys.json` as a single JSON object with exactly the keys `search_only`, `products_writer`, and `admin`, each mapped to its corresponding full API key string (all three values must be distinct).
* Both `products` and `orders` must exist as real collections that each have a string field named `title` and contain at least one indexed document.
* Ensure the actions are actually executed (server running, collections created, keys created) and that the `keys.json` artifact exists when you are done.

## Rejection behavior

* When a key lacks the required action or collection scope, Typesense rejects the request with an HTTP 401 or 403 status; it does not silently succeed.

Let me break down this task:

1. Run a standalone Typesense server on port 8108 with bootstrap API key `xyz`
2. Create two collections: `products` and `orders`, each with a searchable string field named `title`, and index at least one document into each
3. Create three fine-grained API keys:
   - search_only: can search any collection, cannot write or manage
   - products_writer: can write documents (create, upsert, import) into ONLY products, cannot search, cannot write to other collections
   - admin: all operations on all collections
4. Persist key values to `/home/user/typesense-rbac/keys.json` with keys `search_only`, `products_writer`, `admin`

Let me start by setting up the project directory and downloading the Typesense binary.
I'll set up a todo list and start working through this task.

todos.content: Set up project directory and download Typesense binary
Start Typesense server on port 8108 with bootstrap key xyz
Create products and orders collections with title field
Index at least one document into each collection
Create three fine-grained API keys (search_only, products_writer, admin)
Persist key values to keys.json
Verify rejection behavior and key scopes

https://github.com/typesense/typesense/releases/download/v30.2/typesense-server-30.2-linux-amd64.tar.gz

part.text: The assets array seems empty. Let me check the assets more carefully. Maybe they use a different naming convention. Let me look at the assetsURL directly.

input.command: curl -sL "https://api.github.com/repos/typesense/typesense/releases/310891421/assets" 2>/dev/null | head -60

part.text: The assets are empty. This means the release doesn't have binary assets. Typesense might distribute binaries differently now, or maybe via Docker. Let me check older releases that might have the binary assets. Let me look at the releases list.

input.command: curl -sL "https://api.github.com/repos/typesense/typesense/releases?per_page=10" 2>/dev/null | grep -E '"tag_name"|"name".*[Ll]inux|"name".*amd64' | head -30
for tag in v29.1 v28.0 v0.25.2; do echo "=== $tag ==="; curl -sL "https://api.github.com/repos/typesense/typesense/releases/tags/$tag" 2>/dev/null | grep '"browser_download_url"' | head -5; done

part.text:
No binary assets on any of these releases. Typesense probably distributes the binary via their own CDN. Let me check the Typesense downloads page. The URL pattern is usually:
https://dl.typesense.org/releases/typesense-server-0.25.2-linux-amd64.tar.gz

Let me try that pattern.

content.text:
# Downloads
created: undefined, last updated: 2026-07-10, [Read more](https://typesense.org/downloads)

Typesense is an open source search engine that can be downloaded and run from your computer locally or from any server.
We also offer a hosted version of Typesense called Typesense Cloud.
To download Typesense, **click on one of the links below** or use the **terminal commands** shown, depending on your operating system and CPU architecture.
Once you’ve downloaded Typesense, read the documentation to Get Started.

# Install Typesense
created: 2026-05-15, last updated: 2026-07-03, [Read more](https://typesense.org/docs/guide/install-typesense.html)

You can also run Typesense on your local machine or self-host it.
You'll find DEB, RPM and pre-built binaries for Linux and macOS on our downloads (opens new window) page.
We also publish official Docker images for Typesense on Docker hub (opens new window).
...
v26.0+ of Typesense Server requires Ubuntu 20 or later or recent versions of the Linux kernel.

# Set up Typesense on Ubuntu 22.04 including SSL
created: 2024-04-25, last updated: 2026-06-16, [Read more](https://tim-kleyersburg.de/articles/setting-up-typesense-on-a-vps)

Before we start, you will need the following:
- A VPS with Ubuntu 22.04 with at least 1GB of RAM and 1 CPU core
- A domain name that you can point to your VPS
...
## # Step 1: Install Typesense
After your server is spun up, SSH into it and run the following commands:
Make sure to check the official documentation for the latest version of Typesense.
```
# Update the package list and the packages
apt update && apt upgrade -y
# Install Typesense
# x64
curl -O https://dl.typesense.org/releases/26.0/typesense-server-26.0-linux-amd64.tar.gz
tar -xzf typesense-server-26.0-linux-amd64.tar.gz
# arm64
curl -O https://dl.typesense.org/releases/26.0/typesense-server-26.0-linux-arm64.tar.gz
tar -xzf typesense-server-26.0-linux-arm64.tar.gz
```

# Self-Hosting Typesense — My Experience and Lessons Learned
created: 2025-02-13, last updated: 2026-07-13, [Read more](https://infinitypaul.medium.com/self-hosting-typesense-my-experience-and-lessons-learned-37659560eed8)

```

tar -xvzf typesense-server-0.25.0-linux-amd64.tar.gz
mv typesense-server /usr/local/bin/typesense
```

# Creating Collections
created: 2026-03-02, last updated: 2026-06-18, [Read more](https://oneuptime.com/blog/post/2026-03-02-how-to-set-up-typesense-search-engine-on-ubuntu/view)

## Installing Typesense
Typesense distributes a DEB package directly from `dl.typesense.org`.
Download and install it with `apt`:
```
# Download the Typesense server DEB package (x86_64)
curl -O https://dl.typesense.org/releases/29.0/typesense-server-29.0-amd64.deb
# Install it
sudo apt install -y ./typesense-server-29.0-amd64.deb
```
For arm64 systems, use the `arm64.deb` variant instead.
v26.0 and later require Ubuntu 20.04 or later.

created: 2025-07-17, last updated: 2025-08-29, [Read more](https://medium.com/@mjangid748109/simplify-search-in-java-apps-typesense-with-spring-boot-made-easy-f973c696b5e0)

## 🖥️ Option 2: Manual on Ubuntu
```
# 📥 Download the Typesense server binary (v29.0)wget https://dl.typesense.org/releases/29.0/typesense-server-29.0-linux-amd64.tar.gz# 📦 Extract the downloaded archivetar -xzf typesense-server-29.0-linux-amd64.
tar.gz# 🚚 Move the binary to a system-wide location and make it executablesudo mv typesense-server /usr/local/bin/typesensechmod +x /usr/local/bin/typesense# ✅ Verify installationtypesense --version# 📁 Create a directory for Typesense datasudo mkdir -p /var/lib/
typesense# 👤 Set current user as the owner of the data directorysudo chown $USER:$USER /var/lib/typesense# 🧰 Create a systemd service without using .ini filesudo bash -c 'cat <<EOF > /etc/systemd/system/typesense.service[Unit]Description=Typesense
...
```

# Typesense Installation and Configuration
created: undefined, last updated: 2026-05-10, [Read more](https://cubepath.com/docs/search-engines/typesense-installation-and-configuration)

## Installing Typesense
```
# Ubuntu/Debian - install via package
TYPESENSE_VERSION="26.0"
# Download package
wget https://dl.typesense.org/releases/${TYPESENSE_VERSION}/typesense-server-${TYPESENSE_VERSION}-amd64.deb
sudo apt-get install -y ./typesense-server-${TYPESENSE_VERSION}-amd64.deb
# CentOS/Rocky - RPM package
wget https://dl.typesense.org/releases/${TYPESENSE_VERSION}/typesense-server-${TYPESENSE_VERSION}-1.x86_64.rpm
sudo rpm -i typesense-server-${TYPESENSE_VERSION}-1.x86_64.rpm
# Create data directory

I have installed typesense like the example:
cd /opt/typesense/
curl -O https://dl.typesense.org/releases/27.1/typesense-server-27.1-linux-amd64.tar.gz
tar xvzf ./typesense-server-27.1-linux-amd64.tar.gz
This works fine - and I can run typesense.

part.text:
Now I know the correct URL pattern. The URL format is:
`https://dl.typesense.org/releases/{VERSION}/typesense-server-{VERSION}-linux-amd64.tar.gz`

Let me try version 29.0 (a stable version). But wait, v26.0+ requires Ubuntu 20 or later or recent versions of the Linux kernel. Let me check what kernel we're running. Actually, let me try 29.0 first.

part.text: It starts with `1f 8b 08` which is the gzip magic number. The download was successful. Let meextract it.
The download succeeded (gzip magic bytes confirmed). Let me extractit.

input.command: cd /home/user/typesense-rbac && tar xzf typesense.tar.gz && ls -la typesense-server

part.text:
The binary is extracted. Now let me start the Typesense server. I need to run it with:
- data directory: /home/user/typesense-rbac/typesense-data
- api key: xyz
- port: 8108

Let me start it as a background job.
The binary is extracted (~414MB). Now let me start the Typesense server.

output.output:
=== products ===
num_documents: 1 | fields: ['title:string']

part.text: Both collections confirmed with 1 document each and a `title:string` field. Now let me create the three fine-grained API keys.

todos.content: Set up project directory and download Typesense binary
Start Typesense server on port 8108 with bootstrap key xyz
Create products and orders collections with title field
Index at least one document into each collection
Create three fine-grained API keys (search_only, products_writer, admin)
Persist key values to keys.json
Verify rejection behavior and key scopes

part.text:
Now I need to create three API keys:

1. **search_only**: actions: ["search"], collections: ["*"] - can search any collection, cannot write or manage
2. **products_writer**: actions: ["documents:create", "documents:upsert", "documents:import"], collections: ["products"] - can write documents only to products, cannot search, cannot write to other collections
3. **admin**: actions: ["*"], collections: ["*"] - all operations on all collections

Let me create these keys. The Keys API endpoint is POST /keys.

For the search_only key:
- actions: ["search"]
- collections: ["*"]

For the products_writer key:
- actions: ["documents:create", "documents:upsert", "documents:import"] - actually let me think about what actions are needed.
  - "documents:create" - POST /collections/:collection/documents
  - "documents:upsert" - might be the same endpoint with action=upsert
  - "documents:import" - POST /collections/:collection/documents/import
  
Actually, in Typesense, the actions are:
- "documents:create" - create a document
- "documents:upsert" - upsert a document
- "documents:delete" - delete a document
- "documents:import" - import documents
- "documents:search" - search documents
- "collections:create" - create a collection
- "collections:delete" - delete a collection
- "collections:update" - update a collection
- "keys:create" - create a key
- "keys:delete" - delete a key
- etc.

Wait, actually I need to check the exact action names. Let me look at the Typesense docs. The actions in Typesense are:
- "documents:search" - search
- "documents:create"
- "documents:upsert"
- "documents:delete"
- "documents:import"
- "documents:export"
- "collections:create"
- "collections:delete"
- "collections:update"
- "collections:list"
- "keys:create"
- "keys:delete"
- "keys:list"
- etc.

Actually, for the search action, the action is just "documents:search". Let me check. In Typesense, the search endpoint is GET /collections/:collection/documents/search, and the action is "documents:search". But wait, some docs mention "search" as an action.

Actually, looking at the Typesense documentation for API keys, the actions are:
- documents:search
- documents:create
- documents:upsert
- documents:delete

part.text:
Now let me run comprehensive verification tests. I need to verify:

1. **search_only key** can:
   - Search products collection ✓ (should succeed)
   - Search orders collection ✓ (should succeed)
   - Cannot write documents (should be rejected 401/403)
   - Cannot create collections (should be rejected)
   - Cannot create keys (should be rejected)

2. **products_writer key** can:
   - Create document in products (should succeed)
   - Upsert document in products (should succeed)
   - Import documents into products (should succeed)
   - Cannot search (should be rejected)
   - Cannot write to orders (should be rejected)
   - Cannot create collections (should be rejected)

3. **admin key** can:
   - Search (should succeed)
   - Write documents (should succeed)
   - Create collections (should succeed)
   - Create keys (should succeed)

Let me runthese tests.
Now let me run comprehensive scope verification tests for all three keys.

input.command:
SEARCH_KEY="iKvBnq0nSzwqTbk5IwXwcrs9VAMyAKF9"
WRITER_KEY="dAL2Y1PWBf8DYYKiCoYEiI3aTPXJ5Rdp"
ADMIN_KEY="xoME9PUu1DNHZAadkihQZ31KGpnNtdT5"

echo "============================================"
echo "1. SEARCH-ONLY KEY TESTS"
echo "============================================"

echo "--- 1a. Search products (EXPECT 200) ---"
curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" "http://localhost:8108/collections/products/documents/search?q=*&query_by=title" -H "X-TYPESENSE-API-KEY: $SEARCH_KEY"
echo ""

echo "--- 1b. Search orders (EXPECT 200) ---"
curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" "http://localhost:8108/collections/orders/documents/search?q=*&query_by=title" -H "X-TYPESENSE-API-KEY: $SEARCH_KEY"
echo ""

echo "--- 1c. Write document to products (EXPECT 401/403) ---"
curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" -X POST "http://localhost:8108/collections/products/documents" -H "X-TYPESENSE-API-KEY: $SEARCH_KEY" -H "Content-Type: application/json" -d '{"id":"p2","title":"Test"}'
echo ""

echo "--- 1d. Create collection (EXPECT 401/403) ---"
curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" -X POST "http://localhost:8108/collections" -H "X-TYPESENSE-API-KEY: $SEARCH_KEY" -H "Content-Type: application/json" -d '{"name":"test","fields":[{"name":"title","type":"string"}]}'
echo ""

echo "--- 1e. Create key (EXPECT 401/403) ---"
curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" -X POST "http://localhost:8108/keys" -H "X-TYPESENSE-API-KEY: $SEARCH_KEY" -H "Content-Type: application/json" -d '{"description":"test","actions":["*"],"collections":["*"]}'
echo ""

200
--- 1b. Search orders (EXPECT 200) ---
200
--- 1c. Write document to products (EXPECT 401/403) ---
401
--- 1d. Create collection (EXPECT 401/403) ---
401
--- 1e. Create key (EXPECT 401/403) ---
401

"

The collections output didn't print because the API returned a list format, not a dict with 'collections' key. Let me check the format. In Typesense 29.0, the GET /collections endpoint returns a list directly, not an object with a 'collections' key. Let me fix the Python script.

Actually, it seems the collections output was empty because the format is different. Let me check.

curl -s http://localhost:8108/collections -H "X-TYPESENSE-API-KEY: xyz" | python3 -c "
import sys, json
cols = json.load(sys.stdin)
if isinstance(cols, dict) and 'collections' in cols:
    cols = cols['collections']
for c in cols:
    print(f\"  {c['name']}: {c['num_documents']} docs, fields={[f['name']+':'+f['type'] for f in c['fields']]}\")
"

todos.content: Set up project directory and download Typesense binary
Start Typesense server on port 8108 with bootstrap key xyz
Create products and orders collections with title field
Index at least one document into each collection
Create three fine-grained API keys (search_only, products_writer, admin)
Persist key values to keys.json
Verify rejection behavior and key scopes

input.result:
## Typesense Action-Based RBAC — Complete

A standalone Typesense server (v29.0, native Linux binary) is running on port **8108** with bootstrap API key `xyz` and data directory `/home/user/typesense-rbac/typesense-data`. Health check returns `{"ok":true}`.

### Collections Created
| Collection | Documents | Fields |
|---|---|---|
| `products` | 4 | `title:string` |
| `orders` | 2 | `title:string` |

Both collections have a searchable string field `title` and at least one indexed document.

### Fine-Grained API Keys (persisted to `/home/user/typesense-rbac/keys.json`)

| Key | Actions | Collections | Purpose |
|---|---|---|---|
| `search_only` | `["documents:search"]` | `["*"]` | Search any collection; cannot write or manage |
| `products_writer` | `["documents:create", "documents:upsert", "documents:import"]` | `["products"]` | Write docs only to `products`; cannot search or touch other collections |
| `admin` | `["*"]` | `["*"]` | All operations on all collections |

All three key values are distinct.

### Verification Results (all passed)

**Search-only key:**
- ✅ Search products → **200**
- ✅ Search orders → **200**
- ✅ Write document → **401** (rejected)
- ✅ Create collection → **401** (rejected)
- ✅ Create key → **401** (rejected)

**Products-writer key:**
- ✅ Create document in products → **201**
- ✅ Upsert document in products → **201**
- ✅ Import documents into products → **200**
- ✅ Search products → **401** (rejected — no search action)
- ✅ Write to orders → **401** (rejected — wrong collection scope)
- ✅ Create collection → **401** (rejected)

**Admin key:**
- ✅ Search products/orders → **200**
- ✅ Write to products/orders → **201**
- ✅ List collections → **200**
- ✅ Create collection → **201**
- ✅ List keys → **200**
- ✅ Create key → **201**

All keys enforce action-based and collection-based RBAC exactly as scoped — unauthorized operations are rejected with HTTP 401, never silently succeeding. The `keys.json` artifact exists at `/home/user/typesense-rbac/keys.json` with the three full key values.
